demographics: I am 37 years old. I am male. I am White. I have never been married. I had 8 or more years of college education (doctorate or professional degree). My family income is $60,000 to $74,999. I am a strong Democrat. I am liberal. I never attend religious services.

events.event_text: At 25, I volunteered for a social justice organization, which opened my eyes to systemic racial disparities. It reinforced my liberal beliefs and drove me to become more actively involved in advocating for change.

Surrounded by a diverse crowd at a protest I organized, I had a spontaneous conversation with a police officer who shared his personal experiences battling racial bias within the force. This ended up being a humbling, eye-opening moment that made me appreciate the complexity and nuances of systemic issues.

At 22, I moved to a low-income, predominantly Black neighborhood for an internship with a community organizing group. The experience opened my eyes to institutional disparities and deepened my commitment to racial justice work.

At 32, I was one of the organizers of a massive protest against police brutality. During the rally, I was arbitrarily arrested along with many others. The experience gave me firsthand insight into the harsh reality of racial profiling and the urgency of our ongoing struggle.

At 27, I was unexpectedly laid off from my job at a local non-profit, despite my dedicated efforts. This event only strengthened my resolve to advocate for workplace fairness and better employee protections.

At 29, I decided to run for a seat on our local school board, driven by my passion to improve education quality and accessibility in our diverse district. I campaigned tirelessly, knocking on doors and listening to residents' concerns, only to lose by a narrow margin. It was a humbling experience, but it has fueled my activism and civic participation ever since.

compressed_summaries.summary: This person, driven by liberal beliefs, volunteered for a social justice organization at 25, becoming actively involved in advocating for racial justice. At 22, they moved to a low-income, predominantly Black neighborhood, deepening their commitment to racial justice work. At 32, they were arrested during a protest they organized, experiencing firsthand the reality of racial profiling.

This person moved to a low-income, predominantly Black neighborhood at 22, committing to racial justice work after witnessing institutional disparities. At 32, they were arrested during a protest against police brutality, experiencing racial profiling firsthand. Despite being unexpectedly laid off from a non-profit at 27, they remained dedicated to advocating for workplace fairness.

persona_card: "Secular and progressive, this 37-year-old White male Democrat, with a doctorate and a strong commitment to racial justice, is deeply distrustful of institutions and open to social outgroups. He advocates for robust economic redistribution and structural explanations for racial inequality, despite a hint of moral traditionalism."
